# 📌 Pull Request Requirements

## 📝 Description

**Fork the repository and create a new branch using the issue number**

- What does this PR do?
- Why is this change necessary?

---

## 🔗 Related Issue

**Link the issue this PR resolves**

Closes #<issue_number>

---

## 🚀 Changes Made

**List all key changes**

- [ ] Added PR template file under `.github/`
- [ ] Structured PR description sections
- [ ] Included checklist for contributors
- [ ] Added screenshot section for UI changes
- [ ] Ensured consistency with contribution guidelines

---

## 🧪 Testing & Validation

**Explain how you tested your changes**

- [ ] Tested locally
- [ ] No breaking changes introduced
- [ ] Existing functionality works as expected
- [ ] Changes align with project standards

---

## 📸 Screenshots (if applicable)

**Add screenshots if UI changes were made**

| Before | After |
| ------ | ----- |
|        |       |

---

## 📋 Checklist

**Ensure all conditions are met before submitting**

- [ ] My code follows the project’s coding standards
- [ ] I have tested my changes thoroughly
- [ ] I have added necessary documentation (if applicable)
- [ ] This PR does not introduce breaking changes
- [ ] I have linked the related issue (`Closes #[issue_number]`)
- [ ] I was assigned to this issue before working on it

---

## ⚠️ Notes for Reviewers

**Add anything reviewers should pay attention to**

- Key areas to review:
- Any known limitations:

---

## 💡 Additional Context

**Any extra info, decisions, or tradeoffs...**

-
